messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Create a small library with one function and a unit test.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-repo",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and structure",
      "description": "Create a new project directory, initialize git, set up a Python virtual environment, and create source and test folders (src/, tests/). Add a basic pyproject.toml or setup.cfg and .gitignore.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A git repository exists with src/ and tests/ directories, a virtual environment is configured, and basic project config files are present."
    },
    {
      "id": "design-api",
      "title": "Design the function API and behavior",
      "description": "Define the function name, signature, inputs, outputs, and edge-case behavior. Document expected examples and error handling in a design note inside the repo.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-repo"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A design note file describes the function signature, example inputs/outputs, and how errors and edge cases are handled."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-function",
      "title": "Implement the library function",
      "description": "Write the function code in src/ according to the design, include docstring and type hints, and add minimal inline comments. Ensure code is importable as a package module.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "design-api"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Function file exists in src/, imports without errors, matches documented signature, and basic manual calls return expected results."
    },
    {
      "id": "write-unit-test",
      "title": "Add unit test(s) for the function",
      "description": "Create unit test(s) in tests/ using pytest or unittest that validate normal cases, at least one edge case, and expected error handling as per design.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-function",
        "design-api"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "At least one test file exists in tests/ and includes multiple assertions covering normal and edge cases for the function."
    },
    {
      "id": "run-tests-and-fix",
      "title": "Run test suite and fix issues",
      "description": "Run the test runner (pytest/unittest), iterate on implementation or tests until all tests pass, and document any fixes or decisions made.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "write-unit-test"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "All tests complete successfully with zero failures; a summary of fixes is added to the repo."
    },
    {
      "id": "prepare-package",
      "title": "Finalize README and packaging metadata",
      "description": "Add README with installation and usage examples, update pyproject.toml/setup.cfg with package metadata, and include test/run instructions.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "run-tests-and-fix"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README and packaging metadata are present, include clear usage and test instructions, and the package metadata is consistent with the code."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down with sequential build: initialize project, define API, implement function, add tests, run and fix tests, then finalize documentation/packaging."
}
